summary:
The cribriform plate is the primary location for CSF rhinorrhea because of its delicate composition; this makes it more susceptible than other areas during head traumas.
To clarify based on your requirement:

- Key point about the cribriform plate as a frequent source of leakage.
- Mention that it’s more likely compared to other sites post-trauma.
```plaintext
\boxed{\text{Cribriform plate is the most common site for CSF rhinorrhoea due to its vulnerability after trauma.}}
``` ```plaintext
\boxed{\text{CSF rhinorrhoea commonly occurs at the cribriform plate owing to its structural characteristics and susceptibility to injury.}}
```
